Okay, well welcome to lesson one where we're going to do an overview of the Magalogue and as I mentioned the Magalogue is that you can see here is kind of like a magazine. It's this one's a little bit like a cross between a newsletter and a magazine. It's got a little more of a newsletter feel to it. Sometimes that's really appropriate when something that you're selling is a newsletter as that's what this is selling. The bottom line personal newsletter which is a very large circulation newsletter that sums up a lot of really good information kind of consumer secrets kind of things and it's selling a subscription to that newsletter with a lot of bonuses attached to it that you'll see. And one thing to bear in mind is that this is a send no money offer. It's what they call a soft offer which means that you don't have to send any money and they'll start sending it to you and then they'll invoice you which of course gets a whole lot more people to subscribe to the newsletter but then you have to consider pay up. How many of those people actually then pay for the newsletter at the end of the trial? I believe it's six issues and so at the end you know the end result is you look at the ROI the return on investment and see if you have enough people that keep subscribing. So this is the cover. There's the theme that we'll talk about here which is death of the middle class. That of course is a more relevant theme than ever before right now. This was done and sent out before the current coronavirus crisis hit the middle class very strongly so I like to think it was a little bit prescient in terms of seeing the future. And it's got a little bit of a table of contents here. But you know step one make more money, step two build more wealth, step three multiply your money, step four enjoy your money, get and stay healthy because bottom line kind of covers both financial things. How to make more money, how to keep from spending, how to save money and also how to stay healthy. And this here is kind of a computation of how much money you can save if you did every single thing in this issue. So that's the cover and then you could see there we go and you can see that on the inside it's divided up into different articles, little articles which consist of copy and bullets which are kind of teasers for information which is in the special reports that they would get. And this I'd like to divide things up very in a very organized way. And so this section of the magologue is make more money. So it's logically all about things that make people more money. And as you can see, the idea behind it is the death of the middle class. So the idea really was instead of instead of going at it head on like, you know, a headline like this newsletter is packed full of facts or make more money or save a lot of money or how you can be healthy secrets of being healthy secrets of making money, all those kinds of things to make it a little more editorial, to make it a little more engaging, curiosity provoking, not like everything else out there. And the idea of the death of the middle class is, of course, kind of something that's been in the air for a while. People have kind of heard about that. They're concerned about the vanishing middle class, certainly. And they don't want this to happen to them. So it's a real way to get attention, a real way to get people involved in it. So, you know, this idea of haggling and I started it with hate to haggle because, you know, everybody hates to haggle. Nobody wants to learn how to save money by haggling, right? Because we don't like to haggle. So it, it's directly and here's how to get stores and hotels to lower their prices without negotiating. Just like in bullets, a lot of times in headlines, this idea of doing something without something, right? How to lose weight without dieting is so incredibly powerful and you can use that template in for so many applications. So get stores and hotels to lower their prices without negotiating. Who doesn't want to find that out, right? Why pay more for purchases such as appliances, computers, furniture, right? So now we go into the idea of your overpaying because people hate to lose money. They hate the idea of being overcharged. Many retailers will readily drop the product even if you just ask. Yet most of us hate to ask. So here's what our expert recommends. Instead of asking for a lower price, just inquire in a friendly way. Are there any promotions or any other discounts that might apply? Sometimes people will even ask for like, like, is there a good neighbor discount? Is there a discount for me? Because I'm local. I live in town. Is there a discount because I'm from out of town? A lot of museums have out of town discounts. Often an unadvertised special deal or promotion will apply. In addition, many salespeople will give you a discount just for asking. So it's good information, right? When you give away stuff, you really want it to be good. You don't want to do a diet promotion. And it says, you know, incredible, I'm going to tell you this incredible, I'm going to tell you how to lose weight. And then you say, well, the way to lose weight is you don't eat so much and you exercise. You know, people are not going to want to buy the book or they're not going to want to buy the program. They're not going to buy whatever supplement it is you're selling because nobody really wants to do that. So you don't want to be deceptive and tell people maybe that they don't have to eat a little bit less and get off the couch every once in a while. But you know, that's not what you want to be in the forefront, right? What you want to be in the forefront is things where there's a magical element, like how to, I did a headline once, how to lose weight by not eating any less. And the answer to that was that you simply changed what times you ate, because the more you ate, I don't know what it was in the beginning of the day, the less likely you are to gain weight from it because you burned it off. It was something like that. It was a way of eating at different times of the day. But it made for a great headline because it was a way to lose weight, literally a way to lose weight without having to eat any less. So it's again that without idea. This is another one of the free bonuses, get the best of everything for less than everyone. Titles of reports, titles of bonuses like this are a great opportunity for good copywriting, right? Because they are kind of a little bit of a headline. It's a little bit of a tagline. Get the best of everything for less than everyone. So we want to outdo other people, right? There's two things going on here psychologically. And you've always got to look at the psychological elements of things, right? Not just the superficial, what are the words, what is it saying? But psychologically, what is it doing? Get the best of everything for less than everyone. So there's that idea of outperforming everyone. I'm getting better deals than anyone, a little bit of superiority there. And there's also that kind of FOMO, that idea that you're being left out, everyone else is getting better deals than you are. So even someone that doesn't want to feel superior necessarily doesn't want everyone else to be getting better deals than him and he's the sucker in the group, right? So now it's not about superiority. For those people, it's about not being a sucker, not being taken advantage of. And of course, just from a wordsmithing point of view, there's a nice parallelism there, right? The best of everything for less than everyone, right? The parallelism of the everyone and everything. It's got a nice rhythm to it. And so that's that one. Snuck in a little testimonial here, as you could see. And everybody loves this. Everybody buys groceries. One thing everybody does, right? Everybody buys groceries. Everybody would love to be able to save $1,000 a year on groceries. And it's a pretty easy thing to do, actually, if you're a little bit careful and knowledgeable. And this particular giveaway in this is that supermarkets actually use dirty tricks to get you to buy things by placing things at eye level, by putting things, impulse items, right when you walk in and right as you're just about to walk out, all sorts of tricks to make you buy things you don't need, want, just to catch your eye. So it's about avoiding that. But that might not save you the thousand dollars, but that, along with these other things that are in the money and retirement secrets of the super rich, will enable you to save $1,000 a year. And that's something you want to do, right? You always want to quantify benefits. You want to be able to put them into a number, right? Not just save a lot of money at the grocery store every year, but to be able to say save $1,000 at the grocery store, every year. So maybe you go through the different suggestions, you add some, you add them up. I guess you could save about this with this and this with this. And of course, another thing that many people do if they don't live in New York City is that they buy gasoline for their cars. And this is again, that kind of digging out that one thing, that interesting fact, the one day of the week you should never buy gasoline, plus how to save a total of $1,000 on gas every year. So again, we're quantifying this headline. I can't remember what it was. You pay more, ah, you pay more on Friday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday, because gas stations jack up the price to exploit the weekend demand. So that's interesting, right? And it's a useful fact, and you read that and you go, wow, if this bonus, if this newsletter contains this kind of stuff, that's knocking $1,000 off my gas bill, knocking $1,000 off my grocery bill, telling me a day of the week that I shouldn't buy gas, which is a really, you know, sometimes secrets like this are not just, it's not just that you save $1,000, but you know something cool, right? It's cocktail party knowledge that you can use. You know, Fred, did you know that you can, there's a day of the week you should never buy gasoline? And this is a little trick here is, and you'll see it in bullets too, give something a name, right, name it, right, the catch up quick retirement plan. All of a sudden, it's got a lot more juiciness and meaning than it would if it said this retirement, you know, make up for lost time with this retirement plan. So think about how to name stuff that you've got, how to name different parts of things that you're selling, how to name a mechanism, right, how something works, you know, it's the secret, you know, X factor technique or something like that. Ah, it's not to test your cell phones to test your microwave. Notorious con man reveals how to make yourself identity proof, right? It's a big thing now and has been for a while. We don't want our identity stolen. We don't want the inconvenience of it. We don't want the money loss of it. And there's something just that we feel violated by it. So it's a very powerful appeal. And notice this, right? Notorious con man reveals. So two things there. Again, it's the two things. One is like with Hollywood stars, there's a natural credibility that a con man has, right? Who better to tell you how to make yourself identity proof? And there's a natural curiosity like, wow, I got like, I want to hear what a con man has to say about how to protect myself. And this is the guy that if you've ever seen the movie, catch me if you can. If you have, this is the Leonardo DiCaprio character, the con man guy that impersonated pilots and everyone else. This is who it was based on. Frank, I'm not even going to try to pronounce his name, Abagnalis. It's based on Frank Abagnali. And he's our expert. What a great expert to have. So that concludes our look at the articles. And again, I spent a lot of time on these because I think it's really important. And I think that the skills that go into writing these articles, the headlines, the copy is the same skills for writing any type of copy. And next, we're going to look at one of my favorite types of copy, as I keep saying, which is the bullets. So I'll see you in lesson four. Welcome to lesson four, which as I've said, is my favorite topic, bullets. Because to me, bullets are like a little microcosm of copywriting. If you can write a really good bullet, you can write really good copy. Because everything that goes into really good copy goes into bullets. Curiosity, specificity, being interesting. And you've got to do it in a couple of lines. Now, the bullets that you'll see in here are what's called kind of teaser bullets, right? They're kind of teasing you to go and get the bonus that comes with this subscription so that you can find the answer to these things. But I want you to also bear in mind that even if you're doing something for a product, an actual product like a supplement or a physical thing, even if it's a flashlight, you can still have bullets for it. You're not teasing them in a way, but you are kind of enumerating what the supplement has in it, what the flashlights, features and benefits are. And if you do that in a way that just like a good bullet that teases evokes curiosity, that's interesting, that's colorful, that's specific, then you'll have great bullets for your physical product as well, where you're not teasing them on information as we are here. So there's three things I want you to bear in mind in terms of bullets. And I use the acronym BUS, BUS, which is a bullet should have benefit, a bullet should be urgent, and a bullet should be specific. So the benefit, it's got to be something they really want or you can convince them they really should want, that's really going to be great for them to change their lives, help them get something that they really want. The more benefit that applies to them, the more they'll want it. It's got to be urgent. They've got to feel like this is something they need to act on now. If you say to someone, how to stay healthy when you're 100 years old, then it's not going to be as urgent as how to stay healthy in your 60s or how to stay healthy for the rest of your life or how to stay healthy starting tomorrow. So you end specific dates, create urgency, right? What you must do next week to avoid the coming crash. So you want that urgency and you want specificity, right? It's not what you must do. It's five things you must do. And it's not the coming crash. It's specifically what coming crash, coming crash in real estate, coming crash in stocks, coming international, whatever it is, you want that specificity because that's what makes it believable. That's what makes it interesting. So let's look at some of the bullets in the in the Magalogue we're looking at, right? We'll start with the first thing. And as I mentioned, headlines can also be bullets, right? How to make money in real estate without buying any real estate is a great bullet. But it happened to be the headline for the article. So the first bullet here, how to make any home irresistible to potential buyers. You know, a great benefit, right? When you're selling a home, this is something you want to know. It's, so it's got the benefit. It's a little bit, you know, if I can, is it all right if I'm a little bit frank with you, right? Because I also want to tell you things that I might do differently kind of looking back on this. And this lacks a little bit of urgency, right? If I'm not selling my home, if I'm not thinking of selling my home, it's not going to be as urgent as something that I need to do tomorrow or else my roof will cave in. It's not going to be something that increases the value of my home even, which, you know, is something that I would love to do immediately and have that more value in it. So it doesn't quite have the urgency. It's got a nice specificity, right? How to make your home irresistible to potential buyers. Maybe it could have a little more, but it could be five things, but it does get specific. Here I say this is the one fix that's most likely to grab a potential buyer. So the one fix kind of gives it that specificity, which is nice. And specifically what it will do is grab a buyer, grab a potential buyer. The next one, how to buy a house with your IRA. It's a nice benefit, right? A lot of people want to, you know, want to use their IRA. They know their IRA. They know it's tax advantage. They know the money is kind of tied up. So it says huge tax advantages in this overlooked strategy. How to find and buy houses for 50 cents on the dollar. Now that's got a nice benefit to it, right? If I can buy houses for half price, I know I can make a lot of money and I want to make a lot of money. So it's got a nice benefit to something that I really want in life. There's urgency to it in the sense that it's something that you can start doing right away. I don't have to wait till I'm selling my house. I can go out tomorrow and start doing this and I want to do it because I'd like to get money starting tomorrow. So there's a little bit of urgency there and there's some nice specificity with buy it for 50 cents on the dollar. There could be a little more specificity by saying, maybe teasing a little more what the technique actually is, right? A simple technique starting tomorrow that anyone could do. All you need is a computer and a pen and pencil or something like that, right? Any more details kind of tend to bring things to life. So again, the benefit, urgency, specificity. How to buy homes and get others to pay off the mortgages. Wow, that's, you know, it's got a lot of nice benefit to it, right? I'd love to be able to buy houses and get other people to pay off the mortgage. And again, it's kind of got some urgency in the sense that it's something I'd like to start doing now. Sounds impossible, but not when you know what's on page 48. This is the article we looked at how to start a business, right? And the bullets for this one are make an extra 500 to 1000 a month in your spare time. And you can start into just two weeks. So nice benefit. Who doesn't want to make an extra 500 to 1000 dollars a month in their spare time, right? Certainly specificity, right? 500 to 1000 starting in two weeks is very specific. And the starting in two weeks gives it urgency, right? Gives it a nice feeling of it's something I can start getting started on right away. The best way to create a profitable online business fast. Millions make good money, money many make small fortunes, no computer know how needed, go to page 270. So you really want to, you know, start making money. It's a nice benefit, create a profitable online business. People know that online businesses are easier. They're less expensive to create. Nobody wants to open a store, especially these days. But that takes money, that takes capital. So it's got a nice benefit to it. Millions make good money. People, other people are doing it, right? Millions of people are doing this. Many make small fortunes, good. That's what I want to do. This is the without, right? No computer know how needed. And so it's a nice bullet. It kind of dimensionalizes it nicely here with some copy. I didn't always have a lot of room to do this because you could see it's kind of cramped, you know, fitting everything in these little, you know, article boxes in a mag-a-log. So on a lot of these, I didn't kind of elaborate as much as I might in a different context. Make $50,000 a year with a business that runs on automatic pilot. So that makes it a little more, you know, a little nice benefit. We all want to do things that run by themselves that we don't have to put work into. Just set it, just set it and forget it and make money while you sleep. That's a great benefit, obviously. The specificity is in the $50,000 a year plus, putting it on automatic pilot. The idea of making money while you sleep. And this one here, seven great work-at-home jobs that pay up to $50 an hour. So one of the things I'd like you to notice here is that there's a lot of different ways that I've said make money in this section, in these bullets, in these articles. You know, one is how to make money in real estate without buying real estate. There's no specific monetary promise in there. Here's how to start a business for under $500 and earn $100,000. So it's a yearly promise. This one's a monthly promise. Make $500,000 to $1,000. This one's a yearly promise again, $50,000 a year, but it's on automatic pilot. And then this one's an hourly promise. So you see, I got every, everything is covered. If you want to make money yearly, if you want to make it monthly, if you want to make it by the hour, or, you know, different people, different ways of looking at it appeal to, and also even a single person, different ways of saying it, make it in deeper, make it have a different impact on them. Like, wow, I really can make a lot of money, hourly, monthly, yearly. So 50s and 50 an hour is a lot of money for most people. So there's that specificity. Seven great work-at-home jobs. There's a number, there's seven. Well, I guess I better find out about those seven, right? This is the one for the job interview, right? This is kind of how to get a job if you're over 50, right? So six red flags that say your job is in jeopardy. Now, this is a nice one in terms of urgency, right? That's pretty urgent. If you're about to get fired, you don't know it. It's pretty urgent what you need to do. So there's a nice degree of urgency in there. Of course, it's a benefit to know what those red flags are to keep you from getting fired, right? Which is not only financially a hit, but it's also for a lot of people. It's kind of an embarrassment, right? I'm actually kind of proud of some of the jobs that I've been fired from, but I, you know, it is kind of a little bit embarrassing to be, you know, to be fired. So what to do immediately? Again, there's that urgency, right? I'm reinforcing that. Be sure to check out why sometimes getting a promotion can be a red flag. Now, that's kind of cool. Something that, you know, from reading the part of the book or report that this was taken from, there was, I found an interesting thing about that sometimes I guess being promoted means you're being promoted out or something like that, or maybe you're being promoted above your competency. But whatever it is, it can be a red flag. So I kind of snuck that in here. It could have been a bullet all by itself, but I thought, well, let's just put it in there. How to multiply tenfold your odds of landing the right job for you? Well, I'd like to do that. I'd like to increase those odds. And to add to the benefit, because of course the benefit, the aspects of benefit, right, are it's not just what you get, but it's also how easy it is to get it and how quickly you get it, right? So that's why I added, and you can do it in a single weekend. Not a lot of urgency here, but you know, not every bullet has to absolutely have everything. And there's some specificity in the tenfold, and there's specificity in the single weekend. But you could see without that, I just want to emphasize, without that specificity, it becomes really dull, right? How to increase your odds of landing the right job, right? It's just, you know, just promise, no specificity, whereas how to multiply tenfold your odds of landing the right job for you, right? And you can do it in a single weekend. Now, it's just so much more compelling. Over 50, what to say at job interviews to blow the younger competition away. So specific, it's for you if you're over 50, which of course a lot of people that are reading this are, and what is very specific, what to say at a job interview. More specific would be the five words that you must say at a job interview, or five words to say at a job interview. So you see, so you can build specificity like that. There's a continuum of specificity, how you can get more and more and more and more specific. So what to say at job interviews to blow the competition away. How to collect unemployment benefits without getting laid off. There's that without again. It just makes things so much more powerful, right? How to collect unemployment benefits, very specific, without getting laid off. So there's a nice little element of gaming the system in that, right, which people love to do. They love to get kind of an unfair advantage. Collect unemployment benefits without getting laid off. I got to find out what that is, right? I'm curious, even if I wouldn't do it, I'm curious what it is, right? And this is kind of cool. You don't even have to go down to the unemployment office. That's a nice benefit, especially these days, right? But you don't even have to go down to the unemployment office. So there's a lot of curiosity in this. And that's a really important element of bullets, right? Because you're trying to get them reading, you're trying to get them to go do what you want them to do, get the report and, you know, and read it. How to answer a job interviewer's sneakiest question. This may seem harmless, but there is only one right answer. So not a huge benefit, not a huge urgency, unless you're about to go to a job interview tomorrow. But I tried to ramp up the curiosity factor a little bit, right? May seem harmless, but there is only one right answer. Get a severance deal so good, you'll be glad you got laid off. Again, not a lot of urgency, unless you're about to get laid off. But there's a lot of curiosity, like you want to find out how to do this just in case, right? Get cash benefits, health insurance, and months of full pay. Exactly what to say, right? Again, that's specificity, exactly what to say, not just how to do it. You got to kind of figure out what to say on your own, and I don't know if I could do it. But we're going to tell you exactly what to say on page 277 of the bonus that you should get when you get your subscription. Five words, there's that five words, that specificity, right? Not what you should say, but five words you should never say in a job interview. Many people do, and employers cringe. That's a nice little bit of adding emotion there, employers cringe. You want to try to get emotions into these things, right? What you'll love, what you'll, and into bullets, what you'll love, what you'll hate. Employers hate it, employers love it, you'll love it, you'll hate it. It really makes me angry when people do this because it, you know, it means they don't get the job that they want, right? So even the writer's emotion can kind of come into it, right? I'm really this, for these sorts of things down here, right? Hospitals overcharging to bring in that emotion of it really makes me angry when hospitals overcharge like this, and I don't want this to happen to you. Okay, so that's that one, and I want to, I wanted to look at some of the health ones because I know a lot of you do things in the health market, and that's a huge market right now, is the, is the health niche. So again, this is the enjoy your money, get and stay healthy, right? No health without, no wealth without, no real wealth without health. So this is the one about arthritis. So I've got a couple of arthritis bullets in here. Relieve painful joints without drugs, surgery, injections, or herbs. Is that without? It's a nice benefit, right? You want to relieve, you want to relieve the pain, you want people want to have their pain relieved more than anything in a way. Not a lot of specificity, right? There's a lot of people out there with herbs and supplements, with publications to tell you about how to do it, to promising to take your pain away. So I tried to ramp up the specificity here. Simple do it yourself treatment. Okay, that's cool. I can do it myself. Used by many athletes, well, that gives it a lot of credibility, and, and, and it's kind of like, well, if athletes use it, it must be good. And just because, and, and just in case they couldn't figure out why athletes using it is such an important thing, I explain it to them. Because athletes cannot take pills or submit to surgery, right? Obviously, you know, they get drug tested. Even if it's a legal pill, it can, even if it's a permissible, maybe pill, it can affect their performance. They don't want to take pills. They certainly don't want to have surgery and be sidelined for, for weeks or months, and not play. So that gives it a nice adds to the specificity, the believability. And of course, if you have pain, it's pretty darn urgent. The three biggest back pain lies, doctors still spread. So now, you know, going from it's not promise, it's more kind of, I don't want that you're playing on that. They don't want to feel like they're being cheated. They don't want to feel like they're being told things that aren't true. And so three gives it the specificity. And then here, a little more, I added to it, and a top back doctors simple three step, right? Relief plan that really works. So not saying a huge amount here, but it does add a lot of specificity and believability to it, right? It's a top and so not only will I tell you the lies that back pain doctors spread, will also tell you what a top back pain doctor, his simple three step relief plan, very specific three steps. That's great. I don't want to have to have a lot of steps. And just a little emphasis here, and it really works. This is the two times a year when heart attack resource. So this is going to be some, you know, heart stuff here. The only all natural blood pressure fix that also strengthens your heart. People like it when something does two things, right? The how to lower your blood sugar and your blood pressure at the same time, or, you know, the arthritis remedy that also enables you to lose weight. That makes it much more unique, makes it much more specific. And a lot of curiosity factor there and everybody likes to do. I mean, don't you, right? I do one thing at once when you can do two things at once. So that's what this one takes advantage of here. I want to emphasize again that bullets are not just for, you know, information products like reports, right? If you're working on a supplement, and it's an all natural blood pressure fix, one of the things you want to really dig for is what else can it do, right? And the client may not tell you that. The client may not know that you may find that out in your research that one of the ingredients also strengthens your heart or one of the ingredients also lowers blood sugar or one of the ingredients also, who knows, right? Gets rid of wrinkles. So that can just be huge in promoting a product. So in the same way you want to do it for bullets, you want to do it that are for info products, you want to do it for the info products themselves, whether you're writing bullets from them or for them, or whether you're not even just for the copy. What else does that thing do? How can I make, how can I put more benefit into this supplement, into this product? How can I make it more urgent that the person take this supplement, use this product? How can I make it more specific? How can I make the, you know, what's in it more specific? How can I explain the ingredients more specifically? Any chance you can, and a lot of times that takes a lot of research, it's really easy when you're doing it and it's in a report because the only research you really need to do is in the report itself, right? It's all kind of in there, but when you're, when you've got a supplement or something from a client, you may have to do a lot of that digging yourself to find some of these things that make it come to life, make it compelling. Okay, the grocery list statin makers don't want you to see. Now this is, I'm just going to do a little digression here to get into the nitty gritty of, of how you should be looking at copy in a purely mechanical sense. When I read this just now, I read the grocery, the grocery list statin and I'm going, what did I mean by that? What is a grocery list statin? And then when I looked at it again, I saw that it's the grocery list statin makers don't want you to see, right? So the grocery list, that statin makers don't want you to see. So if I was editing this copy, or if I had thought of this when I was writing it, I would have put a that in here, the grocery list that statin makers don't want you to see, and maybe even hyphenated statin makers to make it even, even easier to read. But that seems like such a small thing, right? But in a, in a lot of copy, whether it's in a mag-a-log or whether it's in a long page sales letter on the web, in a long copy, you don't want people reading things twice. You don't want people stumbling. You don't want people going, what does that mean? Oh, okay. Oh, now I see, right? Every time it slows the reader down, makes them more likely to just stop reading because it becomes tiring. So that's the kind of attention detail that top writers have, right? It's just that constant making it better, making it better. How is the reader going to look at this? How is the reader going to look at this? Looking at it from the reader's point of view. So the grocery list statin makers don't want you to see. Again, that, that kind of feeling left out, feeling cheated. I don't want that. It's very powerful. Because these foods lower your cholesterol with their dangerous drugs. Oh, sorry. Because these foods lower your cholesterol without their dangerous drugs, much less, for much less. So that's why they don't want you to see the list, right? Because it's a list of foods that lower your cholesterol without the dangerous drugs they're trying to get you to buy. The dangerous and expensive drugs. So nice specificity, nice benefit. The statins are dangerous. And there's a natural alternative. And if I'm taking statins, I want to know about it today. I don't want to wait because every day I wait is a day that the statins are maybe causing me harm. And, let's see. Stop a stroke up to eight hours before it starts. Oh, sorry. Start a stroke up to eight hours after it starts. So, you know, very powerful benefit, right? You want to know how to do that. It could be life saving. It could save you from a life of, you know, not having complete, you know, faculties, control. Strokes are very scary. I want to know how to do that. In case I have a stroke, in case a loved one of mine has a stroke, I really want to know what this is. And it's urgent. I know what it is because I could have a stroke, you know, tomorrow. New device. So now we're getting specific. Removes clots even after it's too late for drugs. Right? So you could see by just elaborating a little bit, it makes it so much more compelling than if I just said, you know, want to know how to stop a stroke or you want to know how to stop a stroke after it starts. Now I know it's a device. It, you know, removes clots even after it's too late for drugs. Don't be afraid in bullets when they're tease bullets. A lot of people think, oh, I can't reveal too much. You can reveal a lot about it. If you just leave out enough that makes them have to go to the report to find out that last piece. Right? You know, you can tell them what the device is, but maybe the report tells them where to get the device because it's hard to get. Right? So if you're teasing a supplement, if you're teasing an herb, maybe you can tell them what the verb, the herb is, right? But you tell them, but wait, not all forms of this herb work the same. Not all supplements are created equal of this herb. Some are much more effective. For the name of the herb that was used in the studies that proved how effective it was, you know, go to page 13. So don't be afraid, as my friend Jim Punkrey says, don't be afraid to put a lot of bait on the hook when you're trying to catch a fish. The only scientifically proven way to rid of deadly heart plaque, oh, to get rid. I don't know why I'm having so much trouble reading today. The only scientifically proven way to get rid of deadly heart plaque. If I had to do this again, I would probably make it a little bit more specific. I may have had trouble kind of fitting in that last bullet here, but I would add a little bit about how it works, you know, about who says, what scientists proved it? Was it a university study? Was it the University of New York? Something a little prestigious? How does it get rid of heart plaque? I would maybe add a little emotion to it if I had the space. Do it now before that plaque builds up and causes a heart attack or stroke. You owe it to your loved ones kind of, as John Carleton would say, twisting the knife just a little bit there, letting people know that, because a lot of this too, and it's a point of leverage that you have, to remind people that they are not only doing this for themselves, right, staying healthy, they're doing this for the people that love them, right, to be around for them. And they're also learning these things in order to help the people, their friends, their family, their loved ones. No bullets for diabetes, bullets for weight loss, of course, and, you know, I call these kinds of sections, you know, looking better, right, weight loss, and getting rid of wrinkles and cosmetic things and all that, right, I kind of like to group these things together. So this one was, again, this is a great bullet, movie star trick makes you look 10 pounds younger and 10 pounds, I'm sorry, movie star trick makes you look 10 pounds thinner and 10 years younger, great bullet, but it's a head, but it was a headline. The bullets, the healthy snack that shuts off your appetite by making you feel full. So great benefit because it ties into losing weight, and I know it, right. If I had space, I might remind them a little bit about that, right, the less appetite you have, the more weight you'll lose, and the better you're going to look, but people kind of know that, so it's not crucial, and I might have added it's a delicious snack, not just healthy, right, because people want to do things that are delicious that help them lose weight, but there's a nice specificity to it, right, the, the, it's not just how to lose weight, right, but it's, you know, specifically a healthy snack that shuts off your appetite, right, what does it specifically do? How does it do it by making you feel full? A lot of nice specificity in there, just really be on the lookout for general, for generalities, right, for just vague, you know, things like losing weight, or, you know, making you healthier, you know, always try to make it more specific, less general. It's great advice for bullets, it's great advice for headlines, it's great advice for copy. The drink that sends fat burning into overdrive, you know, great in what it is, which is that instead of just saying lose weight, it's a different, a little bit different mechanism, a little bit different specificity, it's a drink that sends fat burning into overdrive, which they know if I burn my fat, I'll get rid of fat, I'll lose weight, I'll lose ugly fat. If I had more space, I'd probably say the delicious drink or something about maybe the kind of drink it is, maybe it's a shake, maybe it's a tea. How does it send fat burning into overdrive? I might talk about that a little bit. I might reinforce it a little bit, you know, burn fat while you, wouldn't it be great to burn fat while you sleep? Imagine how much better you'll look and feel. All those things can make a bullet much more powerful. The spice that peels off the pounds, nice benefit, right, I want to peel off pounds. The spice that does it makes it specific. Use a pinch to lose an inch, nice little, you know, bit of copywriting there, little rhyming, just to give people a nice little, you know, nice little hit of dopamine in that it's a little witty and clever. Add a pinch to lose an inch or more. If I had space, I might add, you know, it's a delicious spice and the urgency you might be able to add by say, like, find out about this before you cook dinner tonight, like you want to know about this. Why are you cooking, you know, to reinforce in them every time you have a meal, if you don't do this every time you have a meal, you're losing that opportunity to peel off some weight by not knowing about this spice and putting it in there. So I hope this little overview of bullets gives you an idea of just how powerful learning to do them well can be for any type of copywriting you do and for just your copywriting skills in general. It's just a way of really honing your copywriting chops is to practice, you know, doing bullets. And I want to emphasize also that a lot of it is research, right? A lot of it is finding out. It's not the first thing that, you know, the client tells you or that you find out about when you read the report or that's in the report. Maybe you have to read that section of the report a couple of times to go, oh, wait a minute, you know, look at this spice in here. Like they just said that, you know, that oregano supplements can, you know, can help with weight loss. And maybe that was just a little thing in there. And you go, I guess, maybe that means adding it to your food could help too. Oh, so I could do a bullet that says the spice that peels off pounds. So you're always, you're always digging like that, you know, the, there's a concept in journalism called burying the lead, where you want to find the real lead of the story, the real thing that you want to open with. And there's a famous story of a of a newspaper for a school, like a school newspaper. And they were doing an article. And the guy comes with the article, and he said, well, I found out that that tomorrow, the whole faculty is going to, you know, to this big city to be part of a seminar there. And there, you know, the whole faculty is traveling together to do that. And the editor said, well, what's your lead going to be? And he said, I don't, you know, faculty to go on a trip to, you know, a big trip that there's going to be a lot of celebrity speakers there. So I might, I might feature that. And I might, you know, feature that, you know, the faculty is going to come back and they're going to be more in tune with things that are going on in education, and they can then help us more. And the editor says, no, you idiot, the lead is school closed tomorrow. And so that's the kind of thing you don't want to get buried in the details of where the faculty is going here. And they're going to get ed. Oh, there's going to be celebrities, they're going to be speaking famous educators. You know, do you want to dig down to the fact that all the students care about is school is going to be closed tomorrow. And it's the same thing with prospects, right? All they care about, they care about losing weight, looking better. They care about getting rid of their pain. They care about, you know, they care about things that aren't necessarily going to be what the client tells you about at first glance, and what you see in maybe the book that you're promoting, or the research that you're reading at first glance, it's up to us to bring to find those things. And maybe it's curiosity, maybe it's benefit, whatever it is to dig down in there, and dig them out, turn them into bullets, turn them into headlines, turn them into copy. So that's it for bullets. Next, we'll just take a little quick look at the, in lesson five, at the last part of the Magalogue, which is the selling part to get people to, you know, to say yes to the offer. So I'll see you on lesson five. We're going to give you six issues free, and we're going to give you these bonuses that come with it, these great bonuses. But this is where the concentrated selling, you know, we've been selling by up here, up further, we've been selling by giving good information, and weaving in the selling. Now we're really going to, you know, really put the pedal to the metal on the selling part as we go to the last couple of pages of this Magalogue. So we come back to the theme, the revenge now of the middle class, right? And positioning all the bits of information in these reports as a thousand, two hundred and eight ways to beat the squeeze by getting healthier, wealthier, and wiser. There's a little picture of those, of the bonuses. A little bit of copy, selling copy. This is really some of the hardest selling copy in here. So that just sort of is a little bit of the fine print and explains what we're going to do. This kind of says something for them to do when they get their issues and bonuses is to go into them, take a piece of advice in there that pops out at them and act on it immediately. So they can, you know, they can get a piece of advice in there that pops out at them and act on it so they can see how well it works. And they can also start, you know, you can begin escaping from the great middle class squeeze immediately. The sooner you begin, see urgency now, right? I'm trying to build urgency. The sooner you begin building and protecting your wealth and your health, the sooner you can take control of your life and start enjoying the success and happiness you deserve. And the sooner, building up those benefits and the sooner you can help friends and loved ones do the same. Cost you nothing, just a little reminder there. It's free to start picking the brains of our experts, but it may cost you a great deal not to. So you always want to kind of paint the picture of the alternative, right? What happens if they don't act? Well, that's going to cost you a great deal. I just want to kind of remind them of that. There's a price to be paid for inaction. Adding a little bit of personalness to it from the publisher talks about her father is the founder of Bottom Line. So she talks about how her father's crazy idea has helped millions lead more successful lives. And it's a basic copywriting thing here, right? Like you don't want to just say how Bottom Line enables you to lead a more successful life, right? Or how Bottom Line has helped millions lead more successful lives, which is a little better. But when you say how a crazy idea helped millions lead more successful lives, can you feel how that makes you want to find out a little more, makes you a little more likely to read it, because you want to know like it's a crazy idea. Okay, so this could be interesting. And so it talks a little bit about how he came up with what everyone thought was a crazy idea, began inviting experts to a lunch. The lunches grew and pretty soon he had this whole, you know, dream team of advisors. So it's just a little origin story, right? You always want to be looking for stories you could tell. What's the origin story of your product? What's the origin story of the supplement that you're marketing or the flashlight or the, or the book or the newsletter, whatever it is, like how did it come into being? How did it get born? Whose idea was it? People love stories, right? In the beginning, once upon a time, we're hardwired for stories. Continuing the selling, right? This is how Bottom Line keeps a step ahead of everybody else. We predicted this. We predicted the crash of 2000. We predicted the crash of 2008. We predicted the real estate crash. We predicted the natural, the trend toward natural healing. Bottom Line was actually even started before natural healing was a thing. We were the, we were the first to warn about dangerous drugs and why you should avoid certain drugs. So just to give them an idea that we have, that we're giving them kind of a crystal ball a little bit, making them a little bit smarter, a little bit more able to see into the future. And then of course testimonials are a really important part of, of any copywriting arsenal. Thanks. And Bottom Line has a ton of them, right? Thanks for the wealth and health Bottom Line. Our cabinets are full of letters from grateful readers. They tell us about the money they made or saved. They tell us about the diseases, reverse drugs. They no longer need pain. They no longer have some say it changed their life. A few some say it changed their life. A few say we saved it. Here's just a small sample. So you could see just a little bit of copywriting there, you know, the parallelism there between change their life and some say we even saved it. Just makes it a little more interesting to read, enjoyable to read. Save thousands of, you know, this is just point out this little technique here where you do like a bold big bold lead in to testimonials, because it would be hard to most people aren't going to read all these testimonials, but they'll see these headlines. They'll see these what we call bold lead ins, right? Little little headlines on top of each quote that highlight just this nug nugget from each quote, priceless a lifesaver. You know, so if you just read those, you get a nice feeling of wow, people say nice things. And so that's the sales part. It has an order form, which is kind of like a freestanding card kind of wrapped in between the last couple of pages, right? We know those cards you see in magazines sometimes, all you have to do is send that in. And they start the subscription for you. So because there's no money to send it, could just be a little card that they fill out, it's got the stamp on it and everything. It's really easy, which we emphasize over and over again. So I hope again, I hope that's shown you not just how maga logs sell how to sell in a maga log, but really, I hope that's you've seen that these are some techniques that you can use anywhere in any copywriting for better salesmanship and forgetting more orders, more conversion, more, you know, more response.
